infobox.value: is a city in [Washington County](/wiki/Washington_County,_Oregon "Washington County, Oregon"), [Oregon](/wiki/Oregon "Oregon"), United States, which is located in the [Tualatin Valley](/wiki/Tualatin_Valley "Tualatin Valley"). It is the southern anchor to the [Banks–Vernonia State Trail](/wiki/Banks%E2%80%93Vernonia_State_Trail "Banks–Vernonia State Trail"), which is a 21-mile-long (34 km) linear trail popular with bicyclists, hikers, and equestrians. The population was 1,837 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4) The community was named for John and Nancy Banks, who owned a nearby dairy farm.[[5]](#cite_note-cityofbanks-5) Incorporated in 1921, it is located in the western part of the county where [Oregon Route 6](/wiki/Oregon_Route_6 "Oregon Route 6") intersects [Oregon Route 47](/wiki/Oregon_Route_47 "Oregon Route 47").

**Banks** is a city in [Washington County](/wiki/Washington_County,_Oregon "Washington County, Oregon"), [Oregon](/wiki/Oregon "Oregon"), United States, which is located in the [Tualatin Valley](/wiki/Tualatin_Valley "Tualatin Valley"). It is the southern anchor to the [Banks–Vernonia State Trail](/wiki/Banks%E2%80%93Vernonia_State_Trail "Banks–Vernonia State Trail"), which is a 21-mile-long (34 km) linear trail popular with bicyclists, hikers, and equestrians. The population was 1,837 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4) The community was named for John and Nancy Banks, who owned a nearby dairy farm.[[5]](#cite_note-cityofbanks-5) Incorporated in 1921, it is located in the western part of the county where [Oregon Route 6](/wiki/Oregon_Route_6 "Oregon Route 6") intersects [Oregon Route 47](/wiki/Oregon_Route_47 "Oregon Route 47").

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banks,_Oregon&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city has a total area of 0.37 square miles (0.96 km2), all land.[[6]](#cite_note-Gazetteer_files-6)

### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banks,_Oregon&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), Banks had a population of 1,837. The median age was 32.0 years. 30.6% of residents were under the age of 18 and 6.6% of residents were 65 years of age or older. For every 100 females there were 98.4 males, and for every 100 females age 18 and over there were 97.4 males age 18 and over.[[8]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-8)[[9]](#cite_note-Census2020PL-9)

0% of residents lived in urban areas, while 100.0% lived in rural areas.[[10]](#cite_note-Census2020DHC-10)

There were 593 households in Banks, of which 52.3% had children under the age of 18 living in them. Of all households, 57.5% were married-couple households, 16.0% were households with a male householder and no spouse or partner present, and 18.2% were households with a female householder and no spouse or partner present. About 14.9% of all households were made up of individuals and 6.2% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.[[8]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-8)

There were 617 housing units, of which 3.9% were vacant. Among occupied housing units, 75.9% were owner-occupied and 24.1% were renter-occupied. The homeowner vacancy rate was 1.3% and the rental vacancy rate was 4.0%.[[8]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-8)

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banks,_Oregon&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census") of 2010, there were 1,777 people, 553 households, and 445 families living in the city. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 4,802.7 inhabitants per square mile (1,854.3/km2). There were 580 housing units at an average density of 1,567.6 per square mile (605.3/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 92.5% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 0.3% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.6% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 1.2% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.3% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), 1.4% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), and 3.8% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 7.0% of the population.[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4)

There were 553 households, of which 57.1% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 59.5% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 13.4% had a female householder with no husband present, 7.6% had a male householder with no wife present, and 19.5% were non-families. 14.3% of all households were made up of individuals, and 2.5% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 3.21 and the average family size was 3.54.[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4)

The median age in the city was 29.5 years. 37.8% of residents were under the age of 18; 7.4% were between the ages of 18 and 24; 31.7% were from 25 to 44; 19.2% were from 45 to 64; and 3.9% were 65 years of age or older. The gender makeup of the city was 50.6% male and 49.4% female.[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4)

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banks,_Oregon&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the census of 2000, there were 1,286 people, 440 households, and 337 families living in the city. The population density was 3,885.7 inhabitants per square mile (1,500.3/km2). There were 492 housing units at an average density of 1,486.6 per square mile (574.0/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 91.14% White, 0.39% African American, 0.31% Native American, 1.79% Asian, 0.47% Pacific Islander, 2.80% from other races, and 3.11% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 3.81% of the population.[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4)

There were 440 households, out of which 50.7% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 64.1% were married couples living together, 8.6% had a female householder with no husband present, and 23.4% were non-families. 17.7% of all households were made up of individuals, and 3.2% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.92 and the average family size was 3.31.[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4)

In the city, the population was spread out, with 35.8% under the age of 18, 7.8% from 18 to 24, 39.9% from 25 to 44, 12.5% from 45 to 64, and 4.0% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 29 years. For every 100 females, there were 97.5 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 93.4 males.[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4)

The median income for a household in the city was $57,500, and the median income for a family was $61,932. Males had a median income of $42,330 versus $26,818 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the city was $21,354. About 3.1% of families and 3.2% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 2.1% of those under age 18 and 4.6% of those age 65 or over.[[4]](#cite_note-wwwcensusgov-4)

## Schools

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banks,_Oregon&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Schools")]

The city has three public schools, which are administered by the [Banks School District](/w/index.php?title=Banks_School_District&action=edit&redlink=1 "Banks School District (page does not exist)"). The city schools are Banks Elementary School, Banks Junior High School, and [Banks High School](/wiki/Banks_High_School "Banks High School").[[11]](#cite_note-district-11)
